methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_selector('c4a_filter_correlation', ctx, top_k=min(5, ctx.X.shape[1]))
methods.label: Correlation feature filter
methods.r_expr: correlation_filter(X, y, threshold = 0.0, top_k = min(5L, ncol(X)))

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_cow_align', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(16), ctypes.c_int32(2), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Correlation optimized warping

references.contract_note: cowarp provides the external dynamic-programming COW contract used by this gate

methods.label: Crop
methods.r_expr: {start <- ncol(X) %/% 8L; end <- ncol(X) - ncol(X) %/% 8L; crop_transform(X, start = start, end = end)}

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_xcorr_align', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(0), ctypes.c_int32(2), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Cross-correlation alignment

references.contract_note: SciPy supplies the edge-clamped interpolation used for each tested integer shift
nirs4all pads/interpolates back to the input width; c4a exposes the strict finite-difference width p-1 and gates NumPy
prospectr combines the NIR detrend convention with its own wavelength argument contract

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_paired_transfer('c4a_pp_direct_standardization', ctx, ctypes.c_int(1), ctypes.c_double(0.0))
methods.label: Direct standardization
methods.r_expr: {target <- 1.05 * X + 0.1; direct_standardization(X, target, X, fit_intercept = TRUE, ridge = 0.0)}

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_dtw_align', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(0), ctypes.c_int32(0), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Dynamic time warping alignment

references.contract_note: dtw-python provides the DTW path; the parity adapter maps that external path to c4a's fixed-length output by averaging query samples assigned to each reference index

methods.label: FCK static
methods.r_expr: fck_static(X, kernel_size = 5L, filter_orders = c(0.5, 1.0), filter_scales = c(1.0, 2.0))

references.contract_note: nirs4all returns resampled detail coefficients only; c4a/PyWavelets gate the concatenated approximation+detail coefficients

methods.label: IAsLS
methods.r_expr: iasls(X, lam = 1e5, p = 0.01, lam_1 = 1e-4, polyorder = 2L, diff_order = 2L, max_iter = 20L)

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_icoshift_align', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(16), ctypes.c_int32(2), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Icoshift interval alignment

references.contract_note: SciPy supplies the edge-clamped interpolation used inside each C4A interval
sklearn supplies the column-selection transformer; the adapter returns C4A's list-of-intervals contract

methods.label: K-bins stratified
methods.r_expr: kbins_stratified(matrix(y, ncol = 1L), test_size = 0.25, seed = 17, n_bins = 5L, strategy = 'uniform')

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_localized_msc', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(17), ctypes.c_double(1e-12), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Localized MSC

references.contract_note: sklearn supplies the per-window least-squares fit for C4A localized MSC
prospectr supplies the MSC transform over each local C4A window
references.r_expr: {ref <- colMeans(X); out <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow=nrow(X), ncol=ncol(X)); half <- 17L %/% 2L; for (j in seq_len(ncol(X))) { lo <- max(1L, j - half); hi <- min(ncol(X), j + half); tmp <- prospectr::msc(X[, lo:hi, drop=FALSE], ref_spectrum = ref[lo:hi]); out[, j] <- tmp[, j - lo + 1L]; }; out}

references.contract_note: SciPy supplies moving-window sums, but the raw-sum contract is numerically unstable on near-singular windows; sklearn is the strict gate
c4a MSC follows the conventional row-wise reference-spectrum contract used by prospectr
current local nirs4all checkout uses a historical column-regression MSC variant

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_fit_y_transform('c4a_pp_osc', ctx.X, ctx.y, ctypes.c_int32(1), ctypes.c_int(1))
methods.label: OSC

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_paired_transfer('c4a_pp_piecewise_direct_standardization', ctx, ctypes.c_int32(5), ctypes.c_int(1), ctypes.c_double(0.0))
methods.label: Piecewise direct standardization
methods.r_expr: {target <- 1.05 * X + 0.1; piecewise_direct_standardization(X, target, X, window_size = 5L, fit_intercept = TRUE, ridge = 0.0)}

references.contract_note: sklearn supplies each local least-squares transfer model for the C4A PDS contract
pycaltransfer exposes the PDS-PLS variant; C4A's current PiecewiseDirectStandardization contract is local OLS and is gated by sklearn above

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_piecewise_msc', ctx.X, _null_f64_ptr(), ctypes.c_int64(0), ctypes.c_int32(16), ctypes.c_double(1e-12), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Piecewise MSC

references.contract_note: sklearn supplies the per-row least-squares fit for each C4A piecewise MSC interval
prospectr supplies the MSC transform; gated on small matrices because its Armadillo normal-equation solver drifts above 1e-6 on ill-conditioned 16-column windows
references.r_expr: {out <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow=nrow(X), ncol=ncol(X)); for (lo in seq(1L, ncol(X), by=16L)) { hi <- min(ncol(X), lo + 16L - 1L); out[, lo:hi] <- prospectr::msc(X[, lo:hi, drop=FALSE], ref_spectrum = colMeans(X)[lo:hi]); }; out}

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_piecewise_snv', ctx.X, ctypes.c_int32(16), ctypes.c_int32(0), ctypes.c_double(1e-12), fit_X=ctx.X)
methods.label: Piecewise SNV

references.contract_note: nirs4all supplies the SNV transform; the adapter applies it independently to C4A intervals

methods.label: Wavelength resampler
methods.r_expr: {source <- seq(900, 1700, length.out = ncol(X)); target <- seq(source[[1L]], source[[length(source)]], length.out = max(4L, ncol(X) %/% 2L)); resampler(X, source_wavelengths = source, target_wavelengths = target)}

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_paired_transfer('c4a_pp_robust_direct_standardization', ctx, ctypes.c_int(1), ctypes.c_double(0.0), ctypes.c_double(0.9), ctypes.c_int32(2))
methods.label: Robust direct standardization
methods.r_expr: {target <- 1.05 * X + 0.1; robust_direct_standardization(X, target, X, fit_intercept = TRUE, ridge = 0.0, trim_quantile = 0.9, max_iter = 2L)}

references.contract_note: sklearn supplies the OLS fit; the adapter applies C4A's documented residual-trimming contract
prospectr drops boundary columns; parity compares the shared valid interior window
nirs4all does not expose c4a's mirror edge mode; the default scipy edge contract changes boundary samples

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_paired_transfer('c4a_pp_saps', ctx, ctypes.c_int32(3), ctypes.c_double(1.0), ctypes.c_int(1), ctypes.c_double(0.0))
methods.label: Score-augmented projection standardization
methods.r_expr: {target <- 1.05 * X + 0.1; score_augmented_projection_standardization(X, target, X, n_components = 3L, score_weight = 1.0, fit_intercept = TRUE, ridge = 0.0)}

references.contract_note: sklearn supplies the PCA score space and OLS fit used by the SAPS transfer contract
c4a benchmark uses ddof=1 to match prospectr's sample-standard-deviation convention

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_transform('c4a_pp_vsn', ctx.X, ctypes.c_double(1e-12), fit_X=ctx.X)

references.contract_note: nirs4all returns resampled detail coefficients only; c4a/PyWavelets gate the concatenated approximation+detail coefficients

methods.c_prefix: lambda ctx: lambda: c_wavelet_features(ctx, mode=1, entropy=1)
methods.label: Wavelet features

references.contract_note: nirs4all fits per-level PCA blocks; c4a gates the flattened-DWT PCA contract with PyWavelets+sklearn
nirs4all fits per-level SVD blocks; c4a gates the flattened-DWT SVD contract with PyWavelets+sklearn

methods.label: Weighted SNV
methods.r_expr: weighted_snv(X, weights = seq(1, 2, length.out = ncol(X)), ddof = 0L, eps = 1e-12)

references.contract_note: nirs4all leaves sklearn PCA on auto solver, which switches solver on wide matrices; c4a gates the full-SVD PCA contract
